children.heading: Establishment of system and forward funding
children.label: a Establishment of system and forward funding

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish, by regulation adopted in accordance with , a system for the direct funding and support of all Bureau-funded schools. Such system shall allot funds in accordance with . All amounts appropriated for distribution in accordance with this section shall be made available in accordance with paragraph (2).section 2016 of this titlesection 2007 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: shall become available for obligation by the affected schools on July 1 of the fiscal year for which such allotments are appropriated without further action by the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall remain available for obligation through the succeeding fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: publish, not later than July 1 of the fiscal year for which the amounts are appropriated, information indicating the amount of the allotments to be made to each affected school under , of 80 percent of such appropriated amounts; andsection 2007 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: publish, not later than September 30 of such fiscal year, information indicating the amount of the allotments to be made under , from the remaining 20 percent of such appropriated amounts, adjusted to reflect the actual student attendance.section 2007 of this title

children.heading: Overpayments
children.label: C Overpayments
children.text: Any overpayments made to tribal schools shall be returned to the Secretary not later than 30 days after the final determination that the school was overpaid pursuant to this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the cost for any single item acquired does not exceed $15,000;

children.label: ii
children.text: the school board approves the acquisition;

children.label: iii
children.text: the supervisor certifies that the cost is fair and reasonable;

children.label: iv
children.text: the documents relating to the acquisition executed by the supervisor of the school or other school staff cite this paragraph as authority for the acquisition; and

children.label: v
children.text: the acquisition transaction is documented in a journal maintained at the school that clearly identifies when the transaction occurred, the item that was acquired and from whom, the price paid, the quantities acquired, and any other information the supervisor or the school board considers to be relevant.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: B Notice
children.text: Not later than 6 months after , the Secretary shall send notice of the provisions of this paragraph to each supervisor of a Bureau school and associated school board chairperson, the education line officer of each agency and area, and the Bureau division in charge of procurement, at both the local and national levels.January 8, 2002

children.label: i
children.text: determining the application of this paragraph, including the authorization of specific individuals to carry out this paragraph;

children.label: ii
children.text: ensuring that there is at least one such individual at each Bureau facility; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the provision of guidelines on the use of this paragraph and adequate training on such guidelines.

children.label: i
children.text: funds appropriated for the operation of any Bureau-funded school that is closed or consolidated; and

children.label: ii
children.text: funds appropriated for any program that has been curtailed at any Bureau school; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary may waive the application of the provisions of with respect to the closure or consolidation of a school, or the curtailment of a program at a school, during such fiscal year if the funds described in clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A) with respect to such school are used to fund allotments made under for such fiscal year.section 2001(h) of this titlesection 2007 of this title

children.heading: Local financial plans for expenditure of funds
children.label: b Local financial plans for expenditure of funds

children.heading: Plan required
children.label: 1 Plan required
children.text: Each Bureau-operated school that receives an allotment under shall prepare a local financial plan that specifies the manner in which the school will expend the funds made available under the allotment and ensures that the school will meet the accreditation requirements or standards for the school pursuant to .section 2007 of this titlesection 2001 of this title

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 2 Requirement
children.text: A local financial plan under paragraph (1) shall comply with all applicable Federal and tribal laws.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The financial plan for a school under subparagraph (A) shall be prepared by the supervisor of the school in active consultation with the local school board for the school.1So in original. Probably should be “paragraph (1)”.1

children.heading: Authority of school board
children.label: B Authority of school board
children.text: The local school board for each school shall have the authority to ratify, reject, or amend such financial plan and, at the initiative of the local school board or in response to the supervisor of the school, to revise such financial plan to meet needs not foreseen at the time of preparation of the financial plan.

children.label: A
children.text: shall implement the decisions of the school board relating to the financial plan under paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: shall provide the appropriate local union representative of the education employees of the school with copies of proposed financial plans relating to the school and all modifications and proposed modifications to the plans, and at the same time submit such copies to the local school board; and

children.label: C
children.text: may appeal any such action of the local school board to the appropriate education line officer of the Bureau agency by filing a written statement describing the action and the reasons the supervisor believes such action should be overturned.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A copy of each statement filed under paragraph (4)(C) shall be submitted to the local school board and such board shall be afforded an opportunity to respond, in writing, to such appeal.

children.heading: Overturned actions
children.label: B Overturned actions
children.text: After reviewing such written appeal and response, the appropriate education line officer may, for good cause, overturn the action of the local school board.

children.heading: Transmission of determination
children.label: C Transmission of determination
children.text: The appropriate education line officer shall transmit the determination of such appeal in the form of a written opinion to such board and to such supervisor identifying the reasons for overturning such action.

children.heading: Tribal division of education, self-determination grant and contract funds
children.label: c Tribal division of education, self-determination grant and contract funds
children.text: The Secretary may approve applications for funding tribal divisions of education and developing tribal codes of education, from funds made available pursuant to .section 5322(a) of this title

children.heading: Technical assistance and training
children.label: d Technical assistance and training
children.text: In carrying out this section, a local school board may request technical assistance and training from the Secretary, and the Secretary shall, to the maximum extent practicable, provide those services and make appropriate provisions in the budget of the Office for the provision of those services.

children.heading: Summer program of academic and support services
children.label: e Summer program of academic and support services

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A financial plan under subsection (b) for a school may include, at the discretion of the local administrator and the school board of such school, a provision for a summer program of academic and support services for students of the school.

children.heading: Prevention activities
children.label: B Prevention activities
children.text: Any such program may include activities related to the prevention of alcohol and substance abuse.

children.heading: Summer use
children.label: C Summer use
children.text: The Assistant Secretary for Indian Affairs shall provide for the use of any such school facility during any summer in which such use is requested.

children.heading: Use of other funds
children.label: 2 Use of other funds
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, funds authorized under the Act of [ et seq.], and this Act may be used to augment the services provided in each summer program at the option, and under the control, of the tribe or Indian controlled school receiving such funds.April 16, 193425 U.S.C. 5342

children.label: A
children.text: provide technical assistance and coordination for any program described in paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: to the extent practicable, encourage the coordination of such programs with any other summer programs that might benefit Indian youth, regardless of the funding source or administrative entity of any such program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: From funds allotted to a Bureau school under , the Secretary shall, if specifically requested by the appropriate tribal governing body, implement a cooperative agreement that is entered into between the tribe, the Bureau, the local school board, and a local public school district that meets the requirements of paragraph (2) and involves the school.section 2007 of this title

children.heading: Terms
children.label: B Terms
children.text: The tribe, the Bureau, the school board, and the local public school district shall determine the terms of an agreement entered into under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: The academic program and curriculum, unless the Bureau school is accredited by a State or regional accrediting entity and would not continue to be so accredited if the agreement encompassed the program and curriculum.

children.label: B
children.text: Support services, including procurement and facilities maintenance.

children.label: C
children.text: Transportation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each agreement entered into under paragraph (1) shall confer a benefit upon the Bureau school commensurate with the burden assumed by the school.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not be construed to require equal expenditures, or an exchange of similar services, by the Bureau school and schools in the school district.

children.heading: Product or result of student projects
children.label: g Product or result of student projects
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, in a case in which there is agreement on action between the superintendent and the school board of a Bureau-funded school, the product or result of a project conducted in whole or in major part by a student may be given to that student upon the completion of such project.

children.heading: Not considered Federal funds
children.label: 1 Not considered Federal funds
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, funds received by a Bureau-funded school under this chapter for education-related activities (not including funds for construction, maintenance, and facilities improvement or repair) shall not be considered Federal funds for the purposes of a matching funds requirement for any Federal program.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: In considering an application from a Bureau-funded school for participation in a program or project that requires matching funds, the entity administering such program or project or awarding such grant shall not give positive or negative weight to such application based solely on the provisions of paragraph (1).
